sanskrit: रजस्तमश्चाभिभूय सत्त्वं भवति भारत । रजः सत्त्वं तमश्चैव तमः सत्त्वं रजस्तथा ॥
hindiTranslation: हे भरतवंशोद्भव अर्जुन  रजोगुण और तमोगुणको दबाकर सत्त्वगुण सत्त्वगुण, और तमोगुणको दबाकर रजोगुण, वैसे ही सत्त्वगुण और रजोगुणको दबाकर तमोगुण बढ़ता है ॥
englishTranslation: Arjuna, when rajas and tamas are subdued, sattva rises. When sattva and tamas are subdued, rajas rises. When sattva and rajas are subdued, tamas rises.
essence: What dominates the mind is temporary, not who you are.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: The three qualities keep replacing one another. When one becomes stronger, the others are pushed down.
simpleInsight: You can stop treating your moods as permanent. What dominates now will not stay forever.
detailedMeaning: On Kurukshetra, Arjuna is still frozen while Krishna explains how the three qualities shape every mind and action. This verse adds the moving pattern: one quality rises only by suppressing the others, showing that inner states are always in flux.
modernRelevance: You wake up sharp, then drift into fog by noon, then feel restless at night. The mind is not one fixed mood — it keeps changing hands.
translationLiteral: रजः (passion) / तमः (inertia) / च (and) / अभिभूय (having overpowered) / सत्त्वम् (purity, clarity) / भवति (becomes) / भारत (Arjuna) / रजः (passion) / सत्त्वम् (purity, clarity) / तमः (inertia) / च (and) / एव (indeed) / तमः (inertia) / सत्त्वम् (purity, clarity) / रजः (passion) / तथा (likewise)
